rulings.Case Title: ALBERT G SWANSON VS. FREDERICK BOST DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 30, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. DEFENDANT PSC SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF WITH THE DECLARATION OF EXPERT WEBB SUPPORTED BY ATTACHED AND CERTIFIED MEDICAL RECORDS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT DEFENDANT PSC COMMITTED ANY NEGLIGENCE OR BREACHED A DUTY TO PROVIDE A SAFE ENVIRONMENT FOR MEDICAL CARE. THE STATEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED EXPERT CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GREG WILSON VS. HARTLAND HOTEL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 30, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS HARTLAND HOTEL AND TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH CAUSATION. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT RAISES AN ISSUE OF COMPARATIVE FAULT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO WILSON DECLARATION OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S REMEDY FOR DISCOVERY COMPLIANCE IS A MOTION TO COMPEL FOLLOWED BY A MOTION FOR SANCTIONS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: YEONG JOO KIM VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 30, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT ONEWEST BANK, FSB, AS THE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (DECLARATORY RELIEF) CC SECTION 1632 (TRANSLATION REQUIREMENT) DOES NOT APPLY TO HOME LOAN MORTGAGES. NO EXCEPTIONS TO THIS RULE ARE ALLEGED. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (B&P CODE SECTION 17200) PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE WRONGFUL CONDUCT OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS, NOT MERELY REFER TO THE DEFENDANTS COLLECTIVELY. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUD) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WITH SPECIFICITY (WHAT WAS SAID OR NOT SAID, BY WHOM, WHEN, AND IN WHAT CONTEXT.) PLAINTIFF MUST DIFFERENTIATE AMONGST THE DEFENDANTS. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION) PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFY THE STATEMENTS, WHEN, AND BY WHICH DEFENDANTS. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AS TO THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEONID PINSKI VS. ALEXANDER VILDERMAN DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Action ;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 30, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER VILDERMAN'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Action IS GRANTED. THE PETITION WAS TIMELY SUBMITTED AND SERVED. THERE IS A STRONG PUBLIC POLICY FAVORING ARBITRATION AND PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT HAS WAIVED HIS RIGHT TO ARBITRATE THIS DISPUTE. DEFENDANT, BY SUBPOENAING DENTAL RECORDS, HAS NOT SUBSTANTIALLY INVOKED THE LITIGATION MACHINERY, AND NO PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF IS SHOWN. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT SUFFICIENTLY COMPLIES WITH CCP SECTION 1295, AND PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT ALLEGES AN ONGOING RELATIONSHIP SUCH THAT THE AGREEMENT APPLIES TO THIS DISPUTE. =(302/CWW)
